originalAlarm.description: `__curr_1644` could be null (null value originating from line 2574) and is dereferenced.

sanitizedDescription: `curr` could be null (null value originating from line 2574) and is dereferenced.
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Same bug as TRANSFORMATION_94: curr from malloc() at L65 can be NULL, dereferenced at L66 (curr->val = rand()%10) without NULL check. Genuine null dereference. No // ERROR at L66, new bug. L65-70 are in main() outside all #ifdef blocks — bug fires in all configs. PC = !CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES AND !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc: requires !SORTFILES (the complementary constraint to TRANSFORMATION_94 which had SORTFILES=T) plus restrictive library macro !_STDLIB_H. PC is over-constrained.
